mapped_actions.description: maker_lab_frontage (transparent maker lab): add a realistic visible maker/co-working interior connected to existing building, wall, door, or window edges.
prototype_display (prototype display wall): add one realistic display zone with abstract diagrams, small models, or material samples anchored to a wall/building frontage or public node.
collaboration_seating (modular bench/worktable): add realistic modular seating and a worktable near existing trees, sidewalk, entrance, or public edge.
smart_bench (charging smart bench): add one realistic smart bench with subtle charging details near a public path or shaded edge.
rain_garden (native rain-garden planting): add native planting that links tree/plant nodes to seating and stormwater edge.
micro_mobility_dock (bike/scooter dock): add a compact micro-mobility dock at the street, entrance, or public-space edge without blocking circulation.
community_screen (interactive community screen): add one restrained screen or panel with abstract graphics only, supporting prototype discussion and public information.

facade_depth_system (3D facade/spatial interface): use wall thickness, doorway depth, canopy, window reveal, platform edge, and ground-plane connection to make the update read as a real three-dimensional spatial scene.

color_block_wayfinding (reversible color accents): add restrained colored blocks on paving, movable furniture, display plinths, or privacy-safe panels to clarify activity zones without readable text or changing building mass.
sparse_activity_scene (limited human activity): add a small believable activity scene with no more than 5 people total, such as discussion, prototype viewing, resting, or slow-mobility arrival.
safe_waterfront_edge (protected waterside relation): add or repair railings, low planting buffers, and non-climbable edges while preserving views to water.
waterfront_roadshow_pocket (small roadshow node): add a compact side-pocket roadshow or demo area with low movable plinths and seating, never blocking the promenade.
ecological_learning_edge (rain garden / water ecology): add native wetland planting, ecology display plinths, and shaded learning seating along safe edges.
slow_mobility_waterfront (bike/arrival edge): add compact bike parking or wayfinding only at side/arrival edges, not on the promenade centerline.

waterfront_material_palette (durable safe materials): use slip-resistant paving, stainless/dark steel railings, timber seating, concrete plinths, perforated metal shade, and planted buffers suited to water exposure.

diverse_street_furniture_materials (function-matched furniture): use varied but coherent street furniture materials and forms matched to the background, circulation, shade, and innovation-community use.

contextual_facade_material_forms (facade material/form diversity): diversify facade or boundary treatment with context-specific materials, depth, openings, screens, shelves, canopies, and planted edges where the existing scene supports them.
vehicle_area (car/vehicle): reduce dominant parked or service vehicles and reconstruct a slow, pedestrian-first innovation-community edge.
closed_boundary (wall/fence): soften the closed boundary and selectively open it as an accessible innovation-community interface.
unsafe_or_passive_waterfront_edge: reduce passive or unsafe waterfront edges while preserving railings, views, promenade continuity, and emergency access.

notes: Observed graph is produced by rerunning OvSGTR on the generated optimized image when available.
Planned graph is synthesized from update plan when no model-generated planned scene graph exists.
